body.title: [sprint] shipped-unproven accepts any non-empty string — structure the lever + recurrence audit
body.description:
intention: I intend that the accumulated missing-proof-levers are a queryable backlog that earns gate removal, not free text lost in close messages.

gate-attack-report.20260707.md G6b, verified at cc_queue.py:1019-1027: the close gate rejects only an EMPTY unproven reason; --shipped-unproven "x" closes a ticket. CLAUDE.md says the accumulated levers ARE the backlog that earns the gates' removal — today they are un-aggregated free text. Shape: (1) lever format nudge: reason should name the missing harness/checker as a noun phrase (soft-validated: reject single-token reasons); (2) levers land in a queryable place (annotation already stored as unproven_reason — add a day-close aggregation view); (3) recurrence rule: same lever named >=2 times -> auto-draft the harness ticket (via the normal filing path, Granny/CC reviewed).

**Affected files:** devlab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (lever soft-validation), skills/day-close-audit/SKILL.md or skills/audit-day/SKILL.md (aggregation + recurrence check)

**Design rules:** CP1/CP2 — unproven closes are the learning signal; visible nuisance drives the lever (CP4)

**Scope boundary:** validation nudge + aggregation + recurrence-draft only; NO hard taxonomy of levers; never blocks an honest close that names a real lever in free words.

**Test plan:** hermetic — close with "x" rejected; close with a named-lever phrase passes; aggregation lists levers by count; a twice-named lever yields a drafted ticket.

**Completion criteria:** single-token lever rejected; day-close prints lever counts; recurrence draft fires in test.

**Validation:** Observable via day-close lever-aggregation output line.

audit-emphasis: none

## Pre-computed constraints (auto-stamped at add time)
[hard_block] ⛔ NO SQLITE. EVER. POSTGRES OR FLAT-FILE ONLY. See banner at top of this file. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[hard_block] Log every state change and every interface crossing. State changes: ticket status — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] No TheIgors imports. UnseenUniversity must be portable without TheIgors present. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] No live keys or passwords in source. `.env` is gitignored. — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] `pip install -e .` must succeed at all times (even with empty stubs). — applies: all (source: CLAUDE.md#hard-rules)
[error] Canonical dev-process memory — one home, no exceptions. Every dev-process artifact — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] device.py and shim.py are the design center. Every component inherits from — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
…and 39 more (lower-severity or capped) — query constraints_get for the full set
body.intention: I intend that the accumulated missing-proof-levers are a queryable backlog that earns gate removal.
